PLANETARY INDICATIONS 

• 

IT IS PRESUMED that these Who undertake to apply 
the information given in Part III have mastered the 
necessary step® of h«roscope making and are familiar 
with the various elements related to the natal and pro- 
gressed horoscopes. 

The degree of success acquired in horoscope reading 
is exactly in proportion to your knowledge of the fore- 
going steps. It is essential, for, accurate delineation, 
that the student be familiar with the tendencies of the 
zodiacal signs, the characteristics of the planets, the 
nature^ of the houses, and the quality of the aspects, 
before "attempting to delineate a horoscope. 

The birth chart (radix or nativity) is like a panoramic 
view of the life as a whole. The indications conta ed 
therein give insight to the character, disposition ;;nd 
tendencies of the individual and what may be expect 'd 
of that person. The progressed chart, containing the 
directional aspects, presents a sectional view of the birt* 
chart and shows the conditions of that particular period 
and the tinges when directional aspects will culminate. 

Life brings conflicting experiences to some persons 
and when reading the indications in a horoscope the 
astrologer sees these, conflictions. For instance, a planet 
in one sign might give contrary testinfony to, that of an- 
other planet in a different sign. One planet signifies a 
certain condition, the other planet seems to deny it or 
testify to the opposite. These two apparently contra- 
dictory 'igdication% do not nullify each other, for the 
testimony oi each planet will manifest when brought 
into action by* a directional aspect or a transit. Some 
influences are nbt brought into play until the latter part 
of life. Every testimony in the bi tfi chart, whether by 
planet, location, aspect or position, regarding character 
of circumstance, will in its own„timf: becolne active. 
This is-jJfeinly foretold in Ecc. Ill, 1-8:

investigators, as well as to provide a practical reference 
work for advanced students and for teachers in class or 
private instruction.

PART III, DELINEATIONS FOR HOROSCOPES 237 


.INFLUENCE OF TAE SUN 
IN THE TWELVE ZOUlACAL SIG*fS 

The Sun 

The Sun apparently passes through all the signs of 
the zodiac in one year, leaving one sign and entering 
the next about the 21st day of each month. The Sun 
is always on tKe ecliptic, therefore, void of latitude. It 
rules the sign Leo and has but one sign for its "home/# 

The warmth which we commonly experience is rot 
the heat of the Sun, as is usually supposed, but is due 
to a cool, positive magnetic wave meeting one of nega- 
tive quality from the Earth, thereby developing both 
light and heat and affecting all other planets likewise, 
in proportion to their mass and density, but vaitying in 
intensity according to the position as related to the solar 
orb and other stellar considerations. 

• 

If not hampered by unfavorable aspects from other 
planets, and dignified by position in the horoscope, it 
bestows a nature which is ambitious, proud, (but seldom 
admitting it) magnanimous, frank, generous, humane, 
firnj and honorable. *Leo natives aspire to positions of 
rulership and. by ^heir earnest nature inspire others with 
a respect for their abilities, so they usually attain posi- 
tions of trust, responsibility and honor where they are 
perfectly at home and capable of practical execution to 
a very satisfactory degree. 

But when the Sun*at birth is unfavorably aspected 
and otherwise undignified the native is inclined to be 
too forceful, lordly, domineering, positive, arrogant and 
extravagant, inclining, also, to sickness of a feverish, 
inflammatory nature, eye afflictions*and heart disorders, 
as well as losses of position, credit or esteenf due to 
impulsiveness^

* Things Ruled by the Sun 

The Son by nature is hot, dry, masculine and life' 
giving. It has much to do with health and the vital 
principle. It has dominion over the individual progress 
and social success. It rules positions of rank and title 
and higii office generally. In the human anatomy it 
governs the sides, back, heart, right eye of the male and 
left eye of the female. * 

Occasionally the Sun may enter the sign a day Earlier or later 
than the dates quoted In this chapter. If a person is born on 
one of jthe date*s given as the change from one sign to another 
It becomes necessary to note carefully the Sun’s place given it. 
birth year ephemeris to determine whether or not it had changed 
signs at time of birth. An ancient rule*was to the effect that 
when a planet reached the 28th degree it began to Influence the 
next sign. 

The planet which rules the sign the Sun occupies at birth is 
known as a "ruling” planet And throughout life the aspects 
of the ruling* planet, whether by transit or by progression, are 
Important. factors in producing events and changing conditions.

THE ASCENbANT l 

* A General Description of the. Influence 
of the Zodiatal Sigh Ascending at *Time of Birth 

The following delineations of the effect of Ascending Signs 
are for the signs alone without any planet in the First House. 
If any planet be therein it will modify these testimonies. A 
fortunate planet will increase the good and diminish the adverse 
qualities; vice versa if the planet be malefic. •• 

* The physical descriptions denoted by the sign ascending may 
be termed the ideal, or normal, which may be expected from 
each sign. In actual practice, however, it will be observed that 
they frequently vary from the normal in some 'respects. The 
variations are mainly due to the effect of the sign in which Is 
located the planet ruling the ascending sign; whether it be in one 
of similar or contrary nature ; the nature of the planets strongly 
aspecting that significator, etc. Racial characteristics, hereditary 
peculiarities, early training, environment, occupational traits, 
and geographical influences, all have some effect in moulding the 
physiognomy. 

The planets which may occupy the First House also have their^ 
share in determining tlfe appearance: Jupiter Inclines to over- 
weight, Saturn to underweight; Mars inclines to develop the 
muscular system ; the Sun’s influence is somewhat similar to 
that of Mars but less florid and more dignified ; Venus denotes 
graceful form and pleasing appearance; Mercury, tall to medium 
height, perceptive »f acuities prominent, % restless activity; Moon, 
rather plump, medium height, pale complexion ; Uranus, physical 
peculiarities, unusual characteristics, odd, impulsive actions; 
Neptune frequently indicates some physical defect, pale or trans- 
parent complexion, something noticeable about the eyes or mouth. 

Saturn ascending usually makes the hair, eyes and complexion 
darker ,than otherwise Indicated. If it is In adverse aspect to 
one of th$ rulers og significator it tends to make the body 
shorter. 

Mars on the Ascendant operates to produce red hair or sandy 
mustache, ruddy complexion and gray eyes. 

^Uranus, Saturn or M*ars ascending usually gives a mark or a 
scar about the head or face, also on that part W the body ruled 
bf the qlgn the planet Is In. 

Uranus alone In the Ascendant conduces to length of limb, 
slender forxff*and peculiar or odd expression, add usually $ large 

i 

The planet ruling hour of birth ‘also has considerable effect on 
the physical and mental make-up of a native *

OENBTHLIACAL ASTROLOGY 


INFLUENCE OF THE PLANETS 
Ilf THE TWELVE ZODIACAL SIGNS 

* '• 

Difference Between Signs and Houses 

r 

One of the stumbling blocks in the path of the be- 
ginner in delineation is to differentiate properly be- 
tween the influence of a planet by sign and house. 

Mediaeval astrologers frequently spoke of the “houses 
of the zodiac,” a term confusing to students, as there 
are no houses in the zodiac, — the signs constitute the 
zodiac. 

A “house” is one of the twelve pyramidal shaped 
divisions of the horoscope, which seem to have their 
apex or\ earth and their outlet in the zodiac. The in- 
fluence of a planet passing through any one of tihese 
divisions is colored both by *he nature of the house 
and sign; consequently it should be read both “on earth 
and in heaven.” 

The, housed deal with things, mundane and material ; 
with the objective mind and environmental affairs. The 
signs govern the (Subjective states of mind, metabolistic 
processes, feelings, tendencies and consequent mertal 
attitudes, originating within the depths of our being and 
which may or may not manifest outwardly on ’the plane 
of action. 

This- chapter deals with the influence of planets by 
sign only.* The suggestions, given at the beginning of 
the chapter entitled, “Influence of Planets in the Mun- 
dane Houses,” page 113, should be carefully read and 
applied ’here. The first and most important step is to be- 
come thoroughly familiar with the intrinsic nature of the 
signs. A planet naturally partakes to some extent of the 
nature of the sign in which it is located, which influence, 
added to its own individual characteristics, produces a 
"blend” calling for fine discrimination in its analysis. 

For instance: The active, enterprising Mars when in 
Capricorn; takes on some of the condensing, combining. 




PART III t DELINEATIONS FOE HOROSCOPES 287 


organizing influence of that sign. The energy of Mars 
is toned dowp by the reserve of Capricorn; the reticence 
of Capricorn is stimulated by the enthusiasm of Mars. 
Hence we see a £ood iJlend of influence resulting in 
the ability to organize and execute matters in a prac- 
tical manner. 

Mars in Libra, however, would not make such a good 
blend of influence. Here Mars is in his detriment. The 
peace-loving, passive Libra, sign of unity and dealings 
with others, would be disturbed by the aggressive, posi- 
tive Mars, indicating ardent, rash, impulsive love affairs, 
difficulty in marriage, strife in pannership and open 
enmities. 

If the nature of each planet and sign is well learned 
there will be little difficulty in reading a horoscope. 


“Every new discovery In physics, chemistry, metaphysics or 
astronomy, simply further astr loglses scientific thought”j*-From 
“Ho# Planets Affect You.”

MOON 

Her Influence In the Twelve Zodiacal Signs 
The Moon Rules the Sign Cancer 

People ruled by the Moon are usually sensitive, emo- 
tional and domesticated, possessing a love for home and 
kindred. They usually are posted regarding the family 
tree, are very patriotic and have a good memory for 
r events in history. They are interested in public condi- 
tions of some sort or other. They are very sympathetic 
and the 4 women are easily led to tears. Having a fertile 
imagination and being also very conscientious, they meet 
with many ups and downs in life. They are quite re- 
ceptive or mediumistic and frequently possess musical 
talent. They like water and natural beauty and should 
never* live for long in a dry, barren place. 

When undeveloped, that is, unenlightened, they may 
be either too "easy going” and given fo s.elf-gratificatiort 
and over-indulgence in eating, drinking or pleasure, or 
are, too changeable, negative and uncertain. 

The developed Lunar individual has passed from the 
brooklet stage to that of “still waters running deep," 
capable of assisting* in floating mighty affairs for public 
need and good. 

As is«the case with Mercurial and Neptunian persons, 
so should Lunarians abstain from animal flesh as food, 
and especially when physically indisposed. The Moon 
represents ‘the stomach and when afflicted leaves its 
natives liable «to diseases incident thereto, and as it is 
said that many o^ the* animals slaughtered for market 
*are diseased, they may be a menace 1;o people with a 
sensitive stomach, as have those ruled bye the Moon. 

. Physifcians say that plenty of wholesome fruit and veget- 
ables will do much to banish worry and .maintain health.

« < •

liquids, stewards^ clerks, companions and other •occupa-

In astrology we refer to the Moon as being cold, 
moist, feminine, fruitfuf and convertible in character, 
that is, being fortunate or unfortunate according to its 
position and aspect's in #, the horoscope. 

It public or common commodities such as gro- 

ceries, liqmd§» etc., that pertain to the domestic side of 
life. Monday is^the day of the Moon. Metal, silver. 

AstrologicaUy Considered 

The Moon exerts considerable influence over # the gen- 
eral or ordinary and common affairs of daily life. It 
makes a complete transit of the zodiac every 27 days, 7 
hours and 43 minutes. In other words, it makes one 
complete revolution through the horoscope of every 
individual in that length of time. As it passes through 
the various "houses" and forms the different "aspects" 
to the planets' places at birth, it produces conditions, 
events, feelings aijd states of mind or health accordingly. 
As no two ‘horoscopes are exactly alike, the Moon's 
monthly transit produces all manner of varying condi- 
tions. 

Things Ruled by the Mqon 

The Moon in ancient times was called Isis, Essqs, 
Luna, Eleusis and the Virgin Mere. She has dominion 
over the ebb and flow of the tides and clear, virgin pure 
water. She is significant of •things of a constantly 
changing or fluctuating character; is feminine, nocturn- 
al, cold, moist, phlegmatic and fruitfcil. She jrules the 
breast and stomach, left eye*of a male and eight eye of 
a female. The Moon rules the public in general, liquids, 
public commodities such as groceries, kitchen utensils; 
fresh water fish, laundry, baking, nursing, bathing, sail- 
ing, brewing, and females generally,

MEpCURY 

Influence *in the* Twelve -Zodiacal Signs 
Mercury rulei' twij signs : Gemini and Virgo 

• In astrology the influence of the planet Mercury is 
known to be variable, convertible, neutral and dualistic. 
That is, it expfesses a nature in accordance' with the 
character of the planet which it aspects: beneflc when' 
with fortunate planets or when in favorable aspect, and 
malefic otherwise. 

Mercury has particular influence over the nervous 
system, bowels, hands, arms, shoulders, collar-bone, 
tongue, sense of sight, perception, understanding, inter- 
pretation and expression. ' 

Persons who respond to Mercury as a ruling planet 
are active minded, quick to discern, eager to acquire 
knowledge, fond of investigation, inquiry ."research, ex- 
ploration and are usually much given to 'reading. If 
Mercury is well placed in the horoscope at birth they 
make good linguists, orators, writers, reporters, teach- 
ers, .secretaries, etc., being fully capable of maintaining 
any position where adaptability, dexterity, perception, 
skill, quick wit, imagination and good memory are re- 
quired. They usually conduct,their work in an orderly, 
methodical, systematic and handy manner, being adept 
at simplifying arrangements. 

But when Mercury is ill placed at birth and attuctea 
the native has the same inclinations as those just men- 
tioned but not the- expert power o$ execution and they 
do better as clerks, printers, bookkeepers, typists, eta., 
carrying out ideas rather than creating them. 

When hfercury is afflicted it inclines to excessive 
’ nervojs activity both of body aqd mind, and^mless con- 
trolled by will power the tendency Is ‘to worry, haste 
and irritability, which inclines to mistakes, forgetfulness 
and controversy. There is alsp a consequent effect on 
the health, detrimental to the ^operation of the bowels

QKfflETHIJACAL ASTROLOGY 


• 

and other organs previously mentioned as^ being under 
this planet’s dominion. At sufch times it will be noticed 
that speech is impede^ by lack* of ability for exp-^sion 
while those wlio are given to stammering will notice 
added impediment. 

The influence of the afflicted Mercury may be much 
improved by cultivating continuity, concentration, de- 
liberation and patience. 

Among the ancients. Mercury was known as Thoth, 
Hermes, and “The ‘Messenger of the Gods.” Its speed 
in orbit ft approximately 95,000 miles per hour. It was 
usually portrayed as a youth, flying with wings at his 
heels, bearing a caduceus made of olive wood about 
which were twined two serpents, the rod being sur- 
mounted with a pair of wings. This symbol well repre- 
sents the essential qualities of the planet, duality,' speed 
and wisdom. It is noticed that Mercurial people have 
a youthful appearance. 

Things Ruled by Mercury 

Mercury rules^that part of the intellect which is sus- 
ceptible to cultivation; it also rufes the sense of sight 
and understanding. It governs the solar plexus, bowels, 
lungs, hands, arms, tongue and the nervous system 
generally. Its colors are blue-gray and dove, also spotted 
mixtures.* Its nature is "neutral and dualistic. Metal, 
quicksilver.

• VfNUS . 

Her influenee in the Twelve Zodiacal Signs 
Venus rules •twoVsigns : Taurus and Libra 

. Venus is fruitful and productive, and is called a fem- 
inine planet as she governs the gentler and more re- 
fined attributes. • Men and women ruled by this planet 
are noticeably kind or sociable. 

When well # aspected in a chart Venus endows the 
native with a pleasant or handsome countenance, sym- 
metrical form and graceful manners. Inclines to har- 
mony, and as she rules the sense of touch, favors art, 
music and decoration. By their pleasing personality 
the subjects of Venus are natural peacemakers; their 
refined'*natures soften the ruffled feelings of friends and 
convert anger to pleasure. As Venus is the promoter 
of pleasure th$se people are splendid entertainers and 
excellent hosts. 

When adversely aspected in the horoscope of birth 
she bestows less beauty and the native is apt to culti- 
vate the social and pleasure-giving tendencies to an ex- 
tent which is detrimental to other Interests, for when 
living too much on the personal plane the real, or finer, 
attributes are submerged and tfee emotions dominate. 

When afflicted Venus produces unsatisfactory do- 
mestic conditions, anxiety in lgve, difficulty with friends 
or through finances. She rules the skin, throat, reins, 
ovaries and internal generative organs, <fnd these* are 
adversely affected by over-indulgence in amusements,^ 
eating and drinkiqg. 

Venus aids tlje faculties of comparison and perception 
as related to order, form, size, weight, color, j^ine and 
time. Also acts on conjugality, an\ativeness, friendship, 
mirthfulness and agreeableness. On tTie days when 
Venus is well aspected by tfie Moon or other pldnets, 
the Activities of those faculties .are accentuated and 
Manifest the best of which the native is then capabtp,

according to the moral status or soul unfoMment, and 
they t are conversely affected in the same 'manner when

The law of the universe is in operation continually 
and manifesting largely through the planetary system 
with both positive and negative forces, which we term 
good and adverse, and it requires effort to maintain 
equilibrium in the counter-balancing elements, but it is 
the effort that brings progressive development. Venus- 
ians particularly inquire that effort to keep their finer 
sensibilities attuned.

MARS 
- • 

His Influence in the Twelve Zodiacal Signs 
Mars rules two signs : Aries and Scorpio 

Mars is significant of energy, the form that energy 
assumes depending upon its position, aspects and loca- 
tion in the horoscope. Manifests as hot, dry £nd mas- 
culine and has nfuch to do with the ambitions, desires 
and animal nature. 

People ruled by Mars are noticeably ambitious, posi- 
tive and fond of leadership. Being quite inventive and 
mechanical they become good designers, builders and 
managers and usually make their way to the front in 
whatever they undertake. 

Being averse to the dictatorship of others and un- 
happy in subordinate positions, they usually find the 
best outlet foe th$ir special abilities when in business 
for themselves or in positions where they can direct the 
work of others. 

On the undeveloped jplane they are tho cruel boss, the 
domineering husband or the masculine wife. But when 
intelligently enlightened and spiritually inclined they 
are excellent healers and teachers and may be found at 
the foremost of movements fpr the advancement of 
public welfare. 

For best resultsMhey jequUe a great deal gf fresh air, 
plenty of water, rest, sleep, music and abstinence from 
highly seasoned foods or stimulating beverages. 

Mars ruled the first month of the Roman yeat 
(March), also the eighth month (October), at .which 
times certain days weft: given over to Martian Ceremon-* 
ies of varioug nature. Until the time of Augustus, Mars 
Iftd bufc two temples at Ronje, and both yrere connected 
with war-like operations. Qne of these was originally 
• only an altar. It was in the Campus Martius, tlft exer- 
cising ground of the army. The other was outside the 


282 


GENETHLIACAL ASTROLOGY 


Porta Caperna, the gate through which* the army march- 
ed on its way to campaigns. 

« 

Every IS y^irs Mars reached its least distance from 
the Earth, i.e., about 36,000,000 miles, when it shines 
with a splendor like that of b£aufiYul Venus. In the 
summers of 1924 and 1939.it reached this proximity. 

« 

Nicolo Marconi, Italian inventor, some years ago 
stated that he had reason to believe th&t messages were 
•being sent to us from Mars. While making experiments 
out on the Mediterranean Sea, his instruments repeated- 
ly received a peculiar call from a wave length 150,000 
miles duration — much larger than any of our instru- 
ments were capable of transmitting at that time. 

Things Ruled by Mars 

o 

Mars rules strength, force t and courage. Its manifes- 
tation is constructive or destructive, according to the 
nature of the aspect|and what use an individual makei 
of the vibrations as compared to his desires, the 
natufe or quality of effort and degree of his understand- 
ing. 

Anatomically considered, Mars governs the external 
sex organs, musculaV system, head, face, left ear, sense 
of taste and the bladder, and these parts afre the most 
readily ajffected with disease when the health has been 
abused o # r neglected. Mars predisposes one to injuries 
through , accident* and hurts by cuts, burns, scalds, 
surgery, firearms, etc., and to f^verisfi or inflammatory 
complaints. Metals governed by Mars are iron and steel.

JUPITER 

His Influence in Jthe Twelve Zodiacal Signs 
Jupiter* ruleg the sign Sagittarius 


• Typical Jupiterians are usually termed jovial owing 
to the fact that Jupiter gives such characteristics as 
sociability, hope, benevolence, veneration, compassion, 
justice, honesty, spirituality, also well developed facul- 
ties of proportion, calculation and location. The Jupiter- 
ian is usually quick to hit the mark, as his •symbol is 
The Archer, and they love out-of-doors exercises; in 
fact, they require considerable of such recreation if they 
would overcome uneasiness and develop poise. 

In |he business world they become interested in large 
popular enterprises and jleal much in matters related 
to lawyers, judges, bankers, brokers and physicians. 
•Science and medicine, insurance a.nd commercial travel- 
ing often engage their attention, also affairs of philan- 
thropic, charitable, religious or benevolent impdirt, as 
well as other matters in the professional world. 

Jupiter is termed “the greater fortune/' and indeed, 
he seems well named, for, unless Jupiter is ill placed in 
the horoscope, he bestows a considerable amount of 
what appears to be good luck upon natives of that sign, 
who are usually fitted for positions of dignity* trust or 
power in business and social circles. ? Having^ logical, 
broad mind, comJideraljJe self-possession, self-confidence 
and determination, they usually inspire^confidence and 
attain responsible positions. 

If Jupiter is not dignified or if adversely aspected ?n 
the horoseope it cayses restlessne^ and uncertainty^ 
giving liability to losses through misjudgmefit, unfor- 
tunate .speculation, investments and trust)#. In the 
human, organism it rules the thighs a/id blood and fre- 
quently causes trouble in ^various Vays through blood 
impurities. To overcome these tendencies, us?* care in 
diet. Dandelion or angelica leaves made into tea is said

the aid of Jupiter for the cure of disease by administer- 
ing medicine in the “planetary hours” ruled by Jupiter. 
Modem physicians, while they have lost the true astro- 
logical import, still invoke Jupiter's influence by mark- 
ing its symbol at the top of their prescriptions, although 
they have changed it somewhat by now making it B 
instead of U as formerly. 

Things Ruled by Jupiter 

Jupiter in nature is said to be warm, moist, sanguine, 
temperate, social, expansive, masculine and moderate. 
Has deminion over the blood, liver, veins, arteries and 
thighs. Rules higher education and philosophic reason- 
ing. If in a person's birth c(iart Jupiter is unafflicted, 
the native will be a good, sound and correct reasoner, 
whereas, if it is ill-dignified or badly aSpected, will often 
exhibit poor, uncertain and unreliable judgment. 

Much depends upon what house this planet occupies In &e 
horoscope. If above the earth at birth *ts influence will manifest 
earlier than when it is found below. It is said that Jupiter’s 
Influence does not affect ithe life with appreciable power until the

SATURN 

His Influence in the twelve Zodiacal Signs * 
Saturn rjules the sign Capricorn 

The influence of Saturn is commonly called evil, and 
in this respect he is much maligned, as in reality there 
is no evil, since all things work together for good 
ultimately. Saturn* acts as a deterrent and because he 
brings denial and necessity into some lives, has been 
considered an oppressor, a Satan. "He that filleth with 
pride will suffer a fall," for Saturn wilt bring hiip to his 
knees, humble bis nature, and by means of restrictions, 
limitations and adversities, will cause the individual to 
ponder, study and seek to find the source of woe, that 
in future it may be overcome. Thus, while Saturn is a 
destroyer (of false ideals), he is also a redeemer, in* that 
he brings the mind to a state of introspection and stim- 
ulates effort toward perfection and victory. 

* 

Persons born’witfi Saturn well placed and aspected in 
their horoscope have a serious and practical nature; 
the)* are wisely economical, prudent, conservative, ex- 
ecutive and profound, b f eing good organisers and man- 
agers. , Their special ability depends upon which planet 
most strongly and favorably aspects Saturn in the chart, 
which might briefly be stated as follows: Moon to Sat- 
urn, successful agriculturists, deajers in land, animals or 
products of the earth; Mercury to Saturn, good writers, 
teachers, scientists; Venus to Saturn, success with large 
corporations, fine property, hi^h-bred stbek; Sun*to Sat- 
urn, municipal or government positions, cTrganizers, 
managers of large concerns ; Mars to Satifrn, engravers 
of metal, masons, contractors, builders, lawyers, sur- 
geons, surveyors^ engineers; Jupiter to Saturn, gain** 
through societies, large companies, religion, science,, 
political office!*, banks? or by speculation in land or 
mines or as # a physician ; Uranus to Saturn, ^practical 
octult science learnings, as # ment^l healing, telepathy, 
osteopathy and drugless methods oS healing, business 
connected with railways, geology, astrology or ^hren- 
plogy; Neptune to Saturn, through unusual vocations. 



302 


GENETHLIACAL ASTROLOGY 


secret service, occupations connected With liquids and 
oils, gains through stocks and speculation: 

Saturnian children are extremely ^sensitive, but they 
hide their feelings and emotions under a mask of re- 
serve. If frequently censured they withdraw from 
association and their progress and development are much 
delayed. 

When, adversely aspected in a chart, Saturn does 
indeed seem to produce a train of adversities, for delays, 
restrictions, disappointments and sorrow are plentiful 
and usually these *ead to misery, poverty and ill-health. 

Saturn governs the knees, teeth, spleen and bones, 
and these parts are most readily afflicted when any 
inharmony prevails. . As his influence is cold and dry, 
heat and moisture are the antidotes. For the native of 
Saturn, critical and important years are those in which 
Saturn will transit in adverse aspect to his radical place, 
that is, between the years 14 to 15, 21 to 22, 29 to 30, 
36 to 37, 44 to 45, 51 to 52, 59 to 60, etc. Avoid new 
undertakings during these periods, rest as much as 
possible, sefek the society of cheerful people and live in 
a light, airy, new place. Persistent metaphysical treat- 
ment, cheerful associates and environment will do much 
to overcome the adverse effects of Saturn's influence.

His Influence in the Twelve Zodiacal Signs 
Uranus rules the sign Aquarius 


Those who are born during that period of the yea-.* 
ruled by .Uranus, or who have Uranus strongly posited 
in their horoscope, are usually attracted to some form 
of occult research, in fact, they are reformers and 
pioneers in advanced lines of thought. It often makes 
one appear odd, eccentric or peculiar and said to be liv- 
ing ahead of their time. It attracts one to such subje ts 
as astrology, phrenology, occultism, mesmerism, mag- 
netic healing, telepathy, electricity, inventions. Free 
Masonry, etc.; in fact, it predisposes to the antiquated, 
curious, new, odd, and everything out of the ordinary. 

When Uranus is well aspected by other planets in the 
nativity, it endows the native with strong ^intuitive ten- 
dencies, giving also metaphysical and inventive ability. 
With' keen foresight they can predict the outcome of 
business or the results of action quickly and with con- 
siderable accuracy in a manner net dependent upon the 
reasoning process, but by knowing. They have strong 
and constructive imagination and can see a way to im- 
prove upon almost everything. Their premonition and 
intuition guide them in «the path of progress and free- 
dom., 

4* < 

When Uranus is adversely aspected* in the horoscope

t explosions, electricity or vehicles, of transportation. All 
the mental malformations can be improved c and accidents 
avoided by those who firmly unite effort with aspiration.

dates on which Uranus is Adversely aspected and care 
then ta!ken to maintain poise and self-control and €o 
avoid risks with the things which Uranus rules.

NEPTUNE 

Its Influence in the Twelve Zodiacal Signs 
Neptune rules the sign, Pisces 

The influence of Neptune upon human kind is that of 
a mcdiumistic order and those who are responsive 6 to 
the vibrations of this mysterious planet are often sub- 
ject to queer and indefinable feelings, sensations and 
emotions. 

At times it produces negative states in which the 
subject takes on the influence of surrounding conditions 
either consciously or unconsciously and acts or feels, 
for the time being, in harmony with the nature of per- 
sons ^contacted. 

When this planet is well aspected in a horoscope, the 
native is endowed with aspiration and correct premon- 
itions and is apparently often directed to do just t«he 
right thing, or to be in just the right place at opportune 
times. 

Such persons are frequently clairvoyant and have 
feelings about* things which should be carefully analyzed 
as Neptune gives psychometric faculty which can be 
developed to so fine an order that its subjects can give 
truly interesting facts through the senS^ of touch and 
feeling, when handling»an article for that purpose. They 
can .also very correctly interpret dreams if they make 
a careful study of that subject. It usually bestows some 
artistic gift, which is ‘susceptible to high development. 

In fact, briefly stated, Neptune represents inspiration, 
psychometry, mediumShip and inner feeling, as Venus, 
'its octave, rules touch or outer feeling. It rules things 
or conditions of'a mysterious, hidden nature, such as 
seance^*, “silence” meetings or affairs of, a secret order. 
Its influence is considered neutral, because the character 
of its expressiqjn is dependent upon the quality of 'the 
aspects made witlf other pl'anets and its position in sign 
and tfbuse of the horoscope. It represents the fee;t in 
the human organism, but in its esoteric sense it govern^

PLUTO 

At the present time very little is yet known of Pluto's 
influence upon human affairs. ' Considering Uranus and 
Neptune (the former discovered iij . 1781, the latter in 
1846) and the length of time which elapsed before man- 
kind began to give visible demonstration of their influ- 
ence in affairs of the world, it is not unreasonable -to 
believe that it will be some years before mankind will be 
able to demonstrate the influence of Pluto in daily life. 

About thirty-fivp years ago astronomers, noting the 
unusual perturbations of Neptune, surmised that a body 
in an orbit beyond it must be causing the disturbance 
of Neptune. A concerted effort was begun to locate the 
disturber. Dr. Percival Lowell built an observatory and 
a 13-inch telescope at Flagstaff, Arizona, especially to 
seek 5ut the planet hitherto unseen by mortal means. 
Unfortunately, he did not live until the planet was 
photographed by a studenf astronomer, W. C. Tom- 
baugh, who located it at last near the fixed star Delva 
Geminorum, that in ancient times was known as Wasat 
and characterized as being of similar nature to Saturn. 
The new planet was therefore in the seventeenth degree 
of the sign Capcer, in the constellation Gemini at time 
of the announcement of its discovery, March 12, 1930. 

* 

At about the same time that astronomers were notic- 
ing the perturbations of Neptune by means of its varia- 
tions on their photographic charts, astrologers were 
likewise noticing otherwise unaccounted for influences 
in charts which’ seemed to focus at about the center of 
the sign Cancer. In fact,’ it is ‘surprising that a planet 
was* not discovered there before that time, as it must 
have appeared on scores of astronomical photographic 
[Slates. However, the position of the big, fixed star, 
Wasat, in that direction complicated the investigations 
of astrologers by causing peculiarities to be assigned to 
it in absence of ocular proof of the presenfe of another 
body there. 

This} in effect, was parallel to the procedure followed 
by astronomers. They noted the behavior of Neptune, 


PART. Ill, DELINEATIONS FOR HOROSCOPE^ 325 


in their charts ancf plates and from the influence exerted 
upon it from some mysterious source* within the solar 
system they realized thye must be another body be- 
yond, which caused the disturbance. This was when 
Neptune was near* the eodiacal conjunction with the 
unseen stranger. 

It is gratifying that astronomy has verified the antici- 
pations of astrologers that a planet would eventually be 
photographed in the Cancer sign of the zodiac. 

It is not taken for granted that th* planet is a new- 
comer in our solar system any more than Urafaus and 
Neptune were newcomers at moment of their discovery. 
Mythology provides plenty of references to justify a 
belief that the ancients were long aware of their pres- 
eyce. Likewise there are references to ultra-Neptunian 
bodies and an intra-Mercurial body named Vulcan. 
Vulcan, however, probably yever will be photographed 
by present-day methods because it is in a spiritualized 
condition with •rarified rays of vibration which do not 
photograph. It has probably passed through all^the 
stages of evolution which the other planets are now 
undergoing and is preparing to enter the “bosom of the 
father*' i.e., return into* the Sun, after w^hich Mercury 
will in* turn succeed to its state as will all the other 
planets in the course of time. A notecl astrologer, L. H. 
Weston, has tomputed the ephemeris of Vulcan and 
many have tested its influence to their satisfaction. As- 
tronomers must develop better means of photography 
before being able to “discover" Vulcan, Jbut the findings 
of astrology are a Significant indication that eventually 
it, too, will be “discovered."

•

will stimulate the older researchers to greater activity 
and will encourage many new students to undertake a 
study of ks effects in horoscc|pes. 'Knowledge concern- 
ing the character or quality of its vibrations will # even- 
tliaUy determine as to whith of the twelve zodiacal 
signs it most closely corresponds, or resembles, and

Pluto ^classical mythology was the god of the outer 
tegior^, the rfiler of the dead. He was # a son of Cronus 
and Rhea, a brother of fceus (Jupiter) and Poseidon 
/Neptune), and at the partition of the world (the king- 
dom of ihe shades fell to him.. He married Persephone 



GENETHLIACAL ASTROLOGY 


(Proserpina). By the Greeks Ke was generally called 
Hades and by the Romans Orcus, Tartarus and Dis 
Pater. As in the oast of all pagan deities; the accounts 
of Pluto vary with different writers and periods. The 
worship of Pluto was extensively spVead among the 
Greeks and Romans. The cypress, Jhu box, the narcis- 
sus, and the plant adiantum (mkiden-hair) were sacred 
(ruled by) to him; oxen and goats were sacrificed to 
him in the shades of night, and his priests were crowned 
with cyprfss. 

He is represented in gloomy majesty, his forehead 
shaded by his hair, and with a thick beard. In his hand 
he holds ar two-forked scepter, a staff or a* key ; by his 
side is Cerberus. He is often accompanied by his wife, 
Persephone. (Cerberus, the dog-monster of Hades is 
described as having three heads, a serpent's tail, and a 
mane o/ snakes. It was subdued by Heracles (Hercules, «, 
son of Zeus) in the twelfth labor. The twelve labors of 
Hercules are believed by manj' writers to represent the 
course of the Sun through the twelve signs of the zodiac. # 

« \ 

In the study of mythology it becomes apparent that 
the ccfrruptidns of time distorted the original meaning 
and indications of the myths. Where originally they 
bore significance^ the heavenly bodies and their move- 
ments, their later treatments and interpretations became 
distorted to suit theological notions. Some careless 
writers confused Plutus, (sometimes pictured as a boy 
with a cornucopia) the Greek god of riches, with Pluto, 
the god of the dead whose oldest name was Hades — "the 
unseen.." # In their, pure rendition the myths were of 
astrological ^ignifftance. 

Th^sons of CVonus and Rhea, that is, Zeus (Jupiter), 
Poseidon (Neptune) and # Pluto, having deposed Cronus, 
ca£t lots for the kingdoms of the heaven, the sea and 
the outer regions. •Jupiter won the heaven,* Neptune 
the sea, and Pluto the outer or infernal regions, which, 
from the original name of their ruler, wqre afterwards, 
known as Hade^. # To some the "house of Hades" was 
a dark and dreadful ^bode or. spirits deep down in the 
earth. But according to another view the "house of 



PART III, DELINEATIONS FOR HOROSCOPES 329 


Hades" was a* land in the far west, which to the Greeks 
was always* thp region of darkness and death; to the east 
was attributed light and life. 

In the oldest Grpek mythology the House of Hades 
was a place neither 'of /eward nor punishment; it was 
simply the home of the dead, good and bad alike, who 
led a dim or shadowy reflection of life on earth ; akin to 
what is now termed the astral plane. 

Pluto was simply the ruler of the house of death; in 
no sense was he depicted as a terapter or seducer of 
mankind, like, the devil of Christian theology. Pluto 
was certainly depicted as stern and pitiless, but he was 
only so in discharge of his duty as custodian of the dead. 

, As it is so evident that mythology has drawn upon 
astrology for its references to the planets, including the 
Sun and Moon, it will nr*t be amiss for astrology to 
draw upon mythology Concerning "the unseen" planet. 
CJranus, under favorable conditions, could be seen with 
the naked eye; Poseidon could be seen wjth a small 
glass or other means available to wise men of old\* but 
Pftito, inhabitant of the "outer regions" was Hades or 
"the unseen." In fact? so inconspicuous* is it that mod- 
ern astronomical photographs recorded it many times 
before astronomers recognized it and realized that it 
was a planef of the "outer region," that is, a wanderer 
in an orbit farthest from the Sun. 

PJuto’s Sign Rulershift 

One of the questions arising since the*rediscovetv of 
Pluto is, "What house does Pluto rule? Mythology 
plainly says in no uncertain terms, "The house of Had£s 
(Pluto) is the hdme of the dead." In. astrology this can 
refer to but one house— the eighth. Th’e eighth* "house" 
in the natural or universal zodiac corresponds t* Scorpio. 

• 

The theological notion plafced the home' of Hades deep 
down in the earth and called it Hell. Mythology desig- 
nated it “in the far west. Astrologically the eighth 




330 1 GENETHLIACAL ASTROLOGY 


house is in the west (west by south). * Astronomically, 
the constellation Scorpio is the abode of the many head- 
ed monster.* 

The three headed dog is usualjy pictured with Pluto; 
said dog is actually pictured in the constellation Scorpio. 
See the three-headed monster above the tail of scorpion 
in the large pictorial diagram (Star Map) of the heavens 
attached Jo back part of this volume. Pluto is “ruler of 
the dead.” In astrology Scorpio and the eighth house 
rule the dead and matters relating to the dead. 

Pluto is usually pictured with a woman ; Scorpio is 
termed a feminine sign. Cerberus is depicted variously 
as having three to one hundred heads, and body covered 
with snakes. Scorpio rules stagnant water and such 
places as are inhabited with scorpions, vipers, etc. 

Pluto — Scorpio 

Until better evidence is found or better reasons ad- 
vanced we believe Pluto rules Scorpio. This displaces 
Mars as ruler of one of the signs to which he has com- 
monly been assigned. Mars is considered hot, dry, 
active ; Scorpio, cold, moist, sluggish. While it requires 
some stretch of* imagination to reconcile the two, there 
are characteristics in common between them sufficient 
to use Mars as rule/ of Scorpio, and we may still con- 
tinue to do so until more is definitely knotvn of Pluto. 

The Nrw Deal, which started shortly after the advent 
of Pluto, indicates the death of many old systems, polit- 
ical and ^economic*. In fact, thousands of banks failed 
in 1932-3, Necessitating a ’complete shut-down of the 
remaining banks and for that period they were “dead.” 
Scorpio, as the eighth sign, is also “ruler of other peo- 
ple's money,” thus banks come under eighth house in- 
fluences. The Blue Eagle insignia, adopted, by the N. 
•R. A., looks strangely like the fantastic bird pictured 
by the ancients as the ruler of Scorpio.

Man has only just now become keen and capable 
enough to “discover” the planet and it will be some time 
yet before he becomes physically and mentally attuned 
sufficiently to manifest any reactions to the influence of 
its vibrations and in the course of time these will devel- 
op the ability to respond to its influence. In the interim 
researchers will be learning something about Pluto by 
means of analogy, thdt is, study of resemblance of prop- 
erties or relations; similarity without identity; reason- 
ing in which from certain observed and known relations 
or resemblances other are inferred; reasoning that pro- 
ceeds from the individual or particular to a co-ordinate 
particulaV, thus involving both induction and deduction. 
In other words analogy is specifically a resemblance of 
relations, a resemblance that rriay be reasoned from, so 
that from the likeness in certain respects \ye may infer * 
that other and perhaps deeper relations exist. In fact, 
that is t iust what we have been doing in this article — 
comparing the known factors of Pluto with its mythx 
ological features and deducing its rulership of the sign 
Scorpio. It may "be called masculine, stern, somewjhat 
inscrutable, not itself .malignant but dealing with high 
potencies ; invoked by or responsive to music,, especially 
the kind of music characterized as “jazz.” 

Scorpio is * Good Sign

Scorpio is equally as good grid as desirable for a birth

and unfavorable indications and in that respect Scorpio 
is npt different from the others. Because it was neces- 
sary to mention things in relation to Pluto and Scorpio 
that* might tend to place the sign in an unfavorable light 
to°new students and to new readers it is only just to 
present sonffe of its better indications. 

Scorpio usually gives strong characteristics, keen 
judgment J^is shrewd, critical, sceptical, tenacious, enter-, 
prising and energetic. Not easily fooled or offended.

but positive; *sd^f-confident, brave and daring. In busi-

Things Related to Pluto 

Pluto rules the underworld, that is, the subconscious workings 
of the body. The fluxing influences, the contest between acid and 
alkali; the fuzing actions; burning out of dross material; all 
those processes whose transmuting actions tend to regenerate the 
body; do sustain, repair, renew or perpetuate it through the burn- 
ing up and casting out of derd material — from death to life. It 
also rules conscience, that inner prompter which helps us judge 
Tight from wropg, subconscious activities, the underworld, stern. 
Incorruptible judges; Without Pluto there would be no subcon- 
scious functions to keep the subtile fires within oui^ being yorking, 
even while we sleep, for our daily repair, renewal, regeneration 
aUd perpetuation; no conscience to give us hell when we do wrong! 
However, this deeper significance of the influence of the planets is 
not always readily discerned. 

About 175 miles south of Cairo on thfc site of the ancient and 
sacred city of ‘Herraopolis a tomb was recently discovered in 
which had been painted scenes depicting the abduction of Fro- 
serpina by Pluto, with Hermes driving the chariot. , In modern 
days this act of Pluto “ruler of the underworld" finds counterpart 
in the acts of “vice barons" taking their chosen victims “for a 
ride" in a gasoline* chari ot. In ancient mythology Pluto is re- 
ferred to as the god of tile underworld, yet a Just? and incorrup- 
tible judge. He passes sentence which gives ttye sorrow or suffer- 
ing that Is due each soul. The sentence and discipline are intended 
to result in strength and purification. Thus death becomes both 
liberator and regenerator. Analysis of the myth leads to the Con- 
clusion that It points to Pluto as “ruler gf the house of death,” 
i.e., the natural eighth sign of the zodiac— Scorpio. , * 

Marvelous as may seem the strides during the eiry, Uranian 
age, they will by*comparison be/dwarfed into insignificance by the 
greatly • preponderant advanc«J undelr ^he 3 influence of Pluto. 
Through comparison and analysis correlating planetary Influences 
and what has gone before in gelation to the developmAt of man 
and his environment, we are drawn logically to the conclusion

Good Aspects of Pluto 

Its good aspects favor the discovery of error, detection of in- 
justice. Revitalizing ideas and incidents which tend to promote 
health, preserve life aqd encourage freer expression of the sub- 
conscious activities are probably due to Pluto's vibrations: also 
beneficial financial prospects, favorable astral or dream exper- 
iences. Good for matters pertaining to healing, cauterizing, pas- 
teurization, fumigation, pest control, surgery, taxes, insurance, 
legacies or affairs connected with the goods or property of the 
deceased. Washing, steaming, steam-baths, preserving, canning, 
hunting, «- embalming, creosoting, painting, purifying, burning 
debris, beneficent auto-suggestion, reproduction, overcoming habits 
or faults and imperfections. 

Adverse Aspects of Pl\ito . 

The adverse* aspects to Pluto incline to inversion of Its better 
qualities, low morality, lack of conscience, Indecency disrespect 
of law, underworld proclivities. Immersed in bondage to appear- 
ance we fail to see the realities or Tncth ; liable to detection in 
error, misdemeanor or crime. Violation of spiritual laws and 
subsequent retribution; Uable to get “stung,” “burnt,” “skinned” 
or apprehended. Possibility of trouble through political conniv- 
ances, tax evasions, misuse of funds, contagion, infection. Unfavor- 
able for matters mentioned under the head of good aspects. Much 
depends upton which planet is adversely aspecting Pluto. In this 
paragraph some of its worst expressions are described, or rather, 
the worst human reactions to its vibrations, c all of which is sub- 
ject to modification, and little of It may be manlf^ttfd by those 
who are making conscious and well directed efforts to convert the 
dross qf self into the gold of Reality.

PART III. DELINEATIONS FOR HOROSCOPES 335 


INFLUENCE* OF THE PLANETS 

IN -THE MUNDANE 'HOUSES 

« 

These interpretations are for the planets by their 
positions in the houses *only, unaspected, unless other- 
wise stated. If a planet is aspected its delineation by 
house, as herein given, is subject to modification corre- 
spondingly. 

Before taking literally, word for word, the delinea- 
tions following, note whether the planet in question is 
in dignity or ^debility ; whether in good or bad aspect, 
nature of aspecting planets and from what houses; then 
modify the reading accordingly. 

For instance, Saturn in the fifth house: Del^y, dis- 
appointment and sorrow through love affairs, affections 
and pleasures; incapacity £or obtaining real enjoyment; 
loss through speculations and investments. 

• 

However, if Saturn were in Capricorn, the sign it 
rules, and well aspected or free from afflictions, it tvould 
b»not nearly so bad, showing an attraction for a serious, 
elderly person ; quiet enjoyment ; success by investment 
in layds, mines and property, lead, coal and such things 
as Saturn rules.. 

By using the foregoing suggestion as a guide, and 
givifig your own modified interpretation,— qualifying, 
amplifying or moderating your deductions to^conform 
with the astrological testimonies, you irtifold ^our latent 
faculties of perception and expression, besides develop- 
ing judgment and intuition. Thus you justify the science 
by proper application of its pVinciples. Consider t^e 
nature of the planet and the nature of the sign in which 
it is located; note whether it is at home, or at ease there- 
in ; then tabulate and study its aspects. With the testi- 
monies thu^ gathered you ye now ready to Mend and 
‘101 * ~ 



GENETHLIACAL ASTROLOGY 


Recapitulation : 

1 — Consider the nature of the planet, whether benefic, 
malefic, neutral, warm, moist, 'cold, fjtc. 

2 — Note the nature of the sign ift which it is posited; 
whether fiery, earthly, airy, watery, cardinal, fixed, com- 
mon, fruitful, barren, positive, negative, masculine, fem- 
inine, northern, southern, human, bestial, mute, 
voiced, etc. 

3 — Note whether, it is at home, or debilitated therein. 

4 — Then calculate its aspects and study each one 
separately. Note the good as well as the adverse aspects. 

5 — ^Jow you are ready to blend and modify the indi- 
cations according to the testimonies thus gathered and 
form a synthesis delineated according to the house 
position.* 

Planets by house position are indications only and do 
not give continued success or continued difficulty. 

c 

For example,. Jupiter in the second or tenth house, 
in good aspect to one or more planets, would give long 
periods of success and prosperity ; but if Jupiter were 
afflicted there would be spells of favorable periods, 
followed by difficult and troublesome times. Just when 
these different conditions would manifest, may be 
judged from the transits and the horoscope by direction, 
/.e., the "Progressed Chart. 

If a planet i£ in good aspect to one planet and in ad- 
verse aspect to another, it indicates success thfough the 
one and misfortune through the other. The two aspects 
do not neutralize each other. Every aspect operates in 
due tim^.

Note: The house position of planets Is mainly 'dependent upon 
the time (hour and minute) of birth. When the time is only 
approximately given, the position of planets in a chart is likewise 
indefinite, especially those near the cusps, when a difference in the 
time might change their position from one house to another. 
Therefore, pay particular attention to the authenticity of the 
given time of birth. If the time Is wrong, the readings for planets 
in houses will be incorrect.

how to foresee conditions -as they really are, for what 4hey are 
worth, and to assist \ in to act In conscious harmony with the 
Law of Nature for best results.’*— From “Powerful Planets.'*

Notice: In this chapter, pertaining to the sixth house of nativ- 
ities. no intention is made to indicate remedies In an advisory 
capacity. Bodily indisposition, as well as mental disturbance, 
should be remedied as soon as possible. Health is important to 
efficiency and happiness and should not be neglected. Therefore, 
when indisposed, it is wise to seek the aid of those who are trained 
In the art of healing and by whatever method of treatment seems 
best, strive to reestablish physical and mental equilibrium.

HOUSE RULERSr 


Significance of a Planetary' House Ruler 
'Vlfhen Posited in Another House 

In the old days delineations such as these were used 
principally for Horary astrology, but if understood and 
used correctly they may be used to being to light ma py ' 
points in the birth horoscope which might othertme 
remain unnoticed. They are suggestions only as to the 
possible effect of the natural house rulers posited in other 
houses, regardless 'of aspects. The influence of aspects 
to each house ruler should be considered 'as a qualifying 
factor. 


A planet is considered in a house as soon as it ap- 
proaches within eight degrees of the degree on thej:usp. 
This is true of all the houses except the Ascendant as 
when a planet has arrived within twelve degrees of the 
ascending degree it is read as though in the first house, 
more especially if it be in the same sign?

to Chart No. 3 on page 170. There it will be seen that

our true selfhood. We suffer mostly from ourselves 
because we have* not learned how best to respond or 
, react to planetary stiftiuli. The overcoming of obstacles 
or rising equal to and abbve conditions is progress. We 
view all things accordjn^ to our state of development; 
we interpret and color them in conformity to our under- 
standing, our character and disposition. 

'"■•‘We are constancy building character; each .day we 
are moulding the future by our reactions to planetary 
conditions, It is the effort to refine and improve our 
reactions which determines our rate <&£ progress and at- 
tainment of happiness. Know Thyself: Wisdom puts 
an end to pain. #

INFLUENCE OF PARS FORTUNJE 
• ■ 

AND 'MOON’S NODES IN 

inE TWELV*E MUNDANE HOUSES 
* * 

0 Moon’s North Ncfde: Dragon’s Head or Caput 
Draconis. 

tf Moon’s South Node : Dragon’s Tail or Cauda 
„ Draconis. 

© The Part of Fortune : Pars Fortunae. 

The point in the zodiac where a planet crosses out 
of south into north latitude is called the North Node; 
the opposite point is the Soutlf Node. 

Some writers believe that the Nodes, and particularly 
the Moon’s Nodes, have no influence in astrology and 
•need not be used, but we have observed many cases 
where the influence of the Nodes was marked. It has 
been observed in progressed horoscopes that when the 
North Node ceached a conjunction with a benefic planet 
in the natal chart benefits of the nature of the house 
occupied were experienced. On the contrary, the South 
Node, or Dragon’s Tail, produced the opposite effect or 
unfavorable conditions. 

Irf Astro-Meteorology the changing node of a superior 
planet, such as Saturn, from north* to south or south to 
north, produces marked changes in the weather; that is 
to say,, had the planet been indicating wet weather it 
then indicates dry weather, and vice versa. 

It requires ninfcteea years, according to its Jnotion of 
approximately three minutts per day, for the Moon’s 
Node to complete the circle of the twelve zodiacal signs. 
It is very interesting to observe* the effects of the Node’s 
influence in nativities, as they transit to a conjunction 
with the Various planets ip the chart. This frequently 
corresponds with important conditions and* circum- 
stances that cannot be accounted for in any dther way. 

‘ © The Part of Ffrtune: Pars *Fortunje 

• Hie Part of Fortune is not a planet. It is a place hi 
the horoscope equally distant ftom the Ascendant as the 


414 ‘GENETHLIACAL ASTROLOGY 


Moon is from 'the Sun at time of birth. C It represents 
worldly success as "indicated by tfy,? house and sign it 
occupies in the nativity. 

i 

It has been said that it is influenced only by conjunc- 
tions and its house position, but in our experience we 
have found a number of cases where directional aspects 
both good and adverse to Pars Fortunae distinctly, 
affected ‘matters ruled by the house- it occupied. 

In one interesting case a lady's natal chart showed 
absolutely no solai 4 indications for marriage, according 
to the regular rules, ye^she was married to a gentleman 
described by the sign in which Pars Fortunae was 
located; and the Part of Fortune was the only point in 
the chart aspected by the Sun.

Fortunae and the North and South Nodes, are liberal transcrip- 
tions from The Doctrine of Nativities, by John Gadbury, 1658.)

PLANETARY ASPECTS 

• A 

"There la nothing either good or bad. 

Bat winking makes It so.” — Shakespeare. 

Truly it is said, “Each star has its own glory.” For 
convenience the influence of planets is termed “good” 
or “adverse” according to the nature of the yibrations 
as tfcey harmonize or conflict with our ideas of what is 
good and what is evil. 

In reality there are no malefic planets or adverse as- 
pects. All things work together for good; but our 
response to planetary vibrations, our manifestation of 
the influence of some of the planets is adverse due to 
^our limited understanding. 

The planets are all heavenly (spiritual) bodies and 
their vibrations manifesting through the various aspects 
commonly tertned “good” and “evil” are interpreted by 
us through our understanding of those terms, which are 
but relative. 

For instance, the Influence of Satura usually called 
“the ‘great malefic," is cold, binding and retarding. Most 
people, when he aspects them adversely, become impa- 
tient at the delays encountered, grow worried or nervous 
and are disappointed to the extent of depression, gloom 
or melancholy, and as these attributes are the reverse 
of what the normal being desires, he terms them 
"malign” and decfdes that Saturn is an* evil planet. 

J'rom different angles (aspects) the nature of a 
planet’s vibrations manifests •differently, the Mfyve 
lengths "being shorter or longer, making direct or indirect 
contacts ; hence, some are pleasing to # our sensibilities 
and some are agitating and tend^ to arrange the chemical 
constituents of the body accordingly. The mi/ld senses 
(usually # $ubcohsciously ) these bodily changes and con- 
ditions, and translates them in terms of thought, hence 
vye are oftentimes mentally elated or depressed without 
realizing the cause, there being apparently no reason for 
the condition. 



420 


(jENE THLIACAL ASTROLOGY 


The human bfedy is a highly sensitive rinetrument and 
subject to rapid changes. It is esp^ci^Hy sensitive to 
atmospheric changes or disturbance. The atmospheric 
conditions are caused by solar, lunar and planetary ac- 
tivity. When we stop to consider hqw^often we breathe, 
how much air is taken into theMungs daily, that there 
is a pressure of air on the body of approximately four- 
teen pounds to the square inch, all of which is in vibra- 
tory activity from planetary sources, we no longer 
wonder at the responses we make and the manifesta- 
tions we give vent to accordingly and in keeping with 
our control (or lack control) of mind and body when 
we are in such close and constant touch. with cosmic 
forces. 

The difference in degree of development or unde* 
standing determines, to a large extent, the difference in 
our response to planetary aspects. He, who is ignoranV 
of Natural Law, will blindly follow his own imperfect 
conception of conditions and then rail at his "bad luck," 
but another under the same influence, who sees above 
and beyond the common acts of daily life, is apt to be 
less unfortunate. The one follows “fate," the other 
anticipates it and profits thereby to the degree of his 
understanding avd refinement of Ms reactions to plane- 
tary vibrations. 

Adverse Aspects Not Evil 

During** adverse aspects we are more apt to see and 
feel the necessity for action to overcome old difficulties 
and obstacles in tHe path of pur progress; apt to feel the 
urge so strongly that a real' anxiety develops to be up 
and doing to safeguard our interests and forestall the 
threatened troubles. Th'is feeling often becomes ner- 
vousness and worry, a form of expression which should 
be*cast aside at oqce as it is a woeful waste of force and 
i loSs of 'power. 

The first essential, then, to transcend adverse vibra- 
tions t is calm the mind with the assurance that “all 
things work together for good." Adverse planetary^ 
aspects viewed from the; standpoint of those who are 



PART HI. DELINEATIONS FOR HOROSCOPES 421 


consciously. nfaWng effort at self-improvement, are con- 
sidered helpful, •fceopuse their driving force produces an 
added urge to press on hardet and faster towards the 
goal of enlightenment %nd achievement. 

It is better to have squares and oppositions in a nativ- 
ity, than to have no aspects, as the square and opposi- 
ticfti certainly give progressive persons great energy and 
ambition, and develop determination to overcome 
obstacles and difficulties. 

By acquiring the right spirit toward life, the right 
philosophy regarding its privileges and opportunities, a 
person may become successful with malefic planets in 
the ascendant. Although beset by strife and difficulty, 
especially in younger years, he may overcome them after 
middle life.*

Planetary Aspects of the Moon D 

The Moon is usually considered cold and moist, fem- 
iyine, negative, receptive, reflective, magnetic, fruitful, 
plastic and changeable. She has a strong influence over 
thctstomach and "digestion, absorption, nutritfon, glands 
and their secretions, the womb, the feminine functions, 
child-bearing, lymph, fat, and the breasts. Also rules 
the brain matter. 

She has to do with occupations and pursuits connected 
with water and liquids generally ; such as sailors, fisher- 
men, brewers, liquor dealers, vessel owners, travelers; 
•with common pursuits and the common classes; also 
nurses, midwives, female servants, women generally, 
female relatives and the mother more particularly. 

•

degrees dexter or sinister. As it is posjible ,for Mercury

Pluto 

• 

£Iot enough is known of Pluto’s influence at this time 
to warrant definite and particular delineation of transit- 
ing aspects to its £lace in the zodiac. Students will do 
well to enter it in every horoscope, and note carefully 
the conditions and events that may consistently be as- 
cribed to its influence. In a preceding chapter is given 
information of its nature. An Ephemeris of Pluto will 
show its location by sign and degree monthly. By 
keeping a daily record of findings on Pluto, you will 
be enabled to form some opinion and judgment of its 
.influence. 


The following .chapter relating to the Influence of the Moon as 
she transits through the twelve houses of a chart is given as an 
example for deducing the influences. The same method or style 
should be employed when treating th£ house transit influence of 
other planets, based upon their delineations given in previous 
chapters. In other wordh, the influence of planets by transit in 
houses is similar to the delineations given previously for the natal 
house’positlons. 

The Moon transits through all the houses of a nativity in about 
twenty-seven and one- third <Jays. 

All the planets affect the house of one’s nativity through which 
they may be transiting. 

The Influence of Transits throygh the natal chart is usually 
more pronounced than through the progressed chart. * 

Mercury, Venus and Sun require about a month to go thorough 
one bouse; Mars about seven weeks, Jupiter about one y&ar; 
Saturn tfbout two and one-half years; Uranus about seven years; 
Neptune requires ^fourteen years to go through one bouse. Pluto 
requires approximately twenty years to pass through a house. 1 
Owing to various reasons the planets may require mcare or less 
time than stated. If an intercepted sign is in any house, the usual 
time may be netfrly doubled.

Influence, of the Moon in the Twelve Houses 

(Regarding the nature ot sign occupied by Moon from 
day to day 9 please refer to note at bottom of page 336.) 

When the Moon is in the Ascendant, or First Hoiise 
of your nativity, it affects the personality and brings 
matters of self into consideration. It brings desfte for 
a change and overcomes conservatism by inclining to 
sociability or publicity. Increases action of moisture in 
head anc^ face. 

In the Second House : Matters of finance will attract 
attention and it is a good time in which to plan methods, 
ways and means of increasing or conducting monetary 
considerations, especially those matters which involve* 
dealings with the public or with commodities of a 
changeable nature. Good for vocal exercise. 

In Third House: Inclines to short journeys, dealings 
with neighbors or kindred and correspondence. If you 
make up your mind about something when Moon is in 
the third house you are very apt t6 change it, especially 
if not in a fixe<f sign. Good for study of public affairs, 
matters of mental enl/ghtenment and breathing exercises, 
as it affects the lungs; favorable for practice requiring 

dexterity of hands and fingers. 

• 

In Fourth House : • Arouses interest in the home, the 
place of .abode and family or domestic .affairs ; also mat- 
ters connected with land 01 property. This location of 
the Moon conduces to thoughts of change, if only (o 
changing things about the house. It affects the breast 
and stomach and one should be careful in eating and 
drinking. 

In Fifth House: Giyis a speculative tendency, an 
inclination to take charices, to favor romance, gaiety and 
the society of younger people. It conduces to happiness, 
mirth, a sense of freedom and > increase of affections. 
Favorable for attending places of amusement. '

In Sixth Hoifee * Conduces to masters of employment, 
employees, labor, food, clothing, animals or sickness. It 
inclines to physical indisposition so that^care should be 
given to hygienic methods. Overwork *or over-indul- 
gence in any way }s likely to result in sickness affecting 
the stomach and bowels. % 

in Seventh House: Awakens interest in matters con- 
nected with partners, associates, marriage, opponents 
and dealings with others. Unions, partnerships, etc., 
undertaken when Moon is in this house are subject to 
changes and therefore care should oe taken not to be- 
stow too much confidence in the* steadfastness Of others, 
or continuity of deals transacted at this time. 

In Eighth House: With the majority of people the 
fr^nsit of Moon through their eighth house is not very 
perceptible in effect, but it inclines to attention of mone- 
tary affairs or financial conditions of others, or the 
money of partner or associate, and causes the mind to 
revert to those who are deceased. It is a good time to 
enter silence for the purpose of psychically communing 
with those in the spirit world. (Choose the planetary 
hoqrs of Moon or Venus.) 

In Ninth House: Turns the attention to higher chan- 
nels of thought, matters of education, spiritual or psy- 
chical unfoldment and thoughts of journeys or reminis- 
cence of distant scenes. Impressive dreams, wheh Moon 
is in this house, are usually prophetic. 

In Tenth House: Incline# to business activity in a 
professional way or with professional* people. It £on- 
dudes to change in business methods or pursuits and 
brings ijp matters connected with honor, credit or ad- 
vancement. • Interest in mother's welfare and also that 
of the Government or President. 

In Eleventh flouse: Usually brings o$e into contact 
with others in a social way, creates feelings oS sociability 
.and interest in friends. *It usually produces new hdjpes 
and wishes and revives old ones. .

In Twelfth House: Gives interest 1 irf occult affairs or 
matters of a secret or mysterious nature, and hospitals

The fqregoing delineations are the combined influences 
of the Moon and the house it is transiting through with- 
out consideration for any aspects which may be formed. 
An aspect would modify or accentuate the conditions as 
stated, according to whether it is favorable or adverse. 



PART III, DELINEATIONS FOR HQROSCOPES 475 


PROGRESSED HOROSCOPE ASPECTS 

The following' delineations have reference to all pro- 
gressed aspects, whether formed to planets in the radix, 
in the progressed 'chart, «r between one planet in the 
radix and the other in the progressed chart. The inter- 
pretations are general indications only and subject to 
modification and change according to the strength of the 
aspect,* i. e., whether major or minor, the position of the 
aspected planets in the chart, whether angular, succedent 
or cadent, and the strength by digirity or weakness by 
debility according to the signs occupied. 

In reading these aspects keep this fact constantly in 
mind, that by direction a planet can only bring to bear 
the influence it indicated in the radix, whether good or 
■otherwise, and in proportion to its power by house, sign, 
aspect, etc., in the radix. For instance, do not predict 
birth of children if the radix denies them. 

It is necessary to understand thoroughly the planet’s 
indications in the radix or its action by direction dannot 
be correctly interpreted. 

Properly speaking, directions are not for the purpose of 
showing what will occur, but whin it will occur, as 
nothing can happen except what the radix indicates. 

If the foregoing is strictly adhered to, directions may 
be easily and coirectly read. 

It should be remembered that mutual aspects may be 
brqught into manifestation by Transits, by Lunar ^pro- 
gressed aspects and by Lunations. 

The delineations given for the progressed aspects will 
also serve as readings for transit aspects and* tor the 
influence of 'parallels of transiting planets.

Note: When planets come Into aspect with their own natal 
place they tend to express what they implied In the radix. They 
influence affairs indicated by the house through which they are 
passing by progression or transit. Wlv'n a progressed or transit- 
ing planet aspecta Its own radical place, insert it in the radix 
chart and note which house therein it is now influencing, and 
make delineation according to the houses occupied. 

Progressed planetary aspects have an orb of only two degrees, 
therefor^ the ascending degree (the degree on cusp of first house) 
must be kqown to be correct, else the progressd planetary aspects 
will not be effective at "the period (year) expected, The transit- 
ing planetary aspects have an orb of eipht degrees to either 
radix or progressed planets, on house cusps.

Note: Although the foregoing delineations 'indicate 
the influence of progressed aspects, they*als/5 serve to 
delineate the aspects <Jf transiting planets. Allow an 
8-degree orb for jnajor aspects by transiting planets. 

When a planet progresses or transits r to conjunction 
of Midheaven, it is then also irf opposifion to cusp of 
fourth house, and square to ascending and descending/ 
cusps providing there are no intercepted signs. Conse- 
quently, a judicious interpretation is required of each 
indication suitable to that particular individual's nature 
and environment, in order to form a correct judgment 
of the combined influences. 


PROGRESSED OR TRANSITING ASPECTS 
TO RADIX PLANETS OR CUSPS 


A planet may progress to an aspect of its own place 
in the radix as illustrated and delineated on £a ge 183. 

To illustrate, suppose that Jupiter is progressing or 
transiting through the fourth house when making a sex- 
tile to its own radical place. We know that the sextile* 
is a benefic aspect *and that the fourth house has to ^o 
with the domestic life, land, property and mines, there- 
fore the aspect would indicate a good period for matters 
relating to those things. If the aspect had been a square, 
instead of U textile, then of course matters relating to 
those things would provfc unfavorable. f 

It should be remembered that if a transiting or pro- 
gressing planet is forming an aspect with a radical 
planet g tfie delineation should be based upon the influ- 
ences of the house positions of these two in the radical 
chart, • 

c But c X sifch planet is forming an aspect to a progressed 
planet, the delineation should be based upon the houses 
occupied by tjie aspfecting planets in the progressedL chart. 

See note on page 494.

RECTIFICATION OF THE BIRTH TIME 
Approximate Birth Tiipes 

These rules are for rectifying a given or approximately 
t known birth time by iheans of events, to find the exact 
•zodiacal degree ascending in the natal chart and to obtain 
the correct time of birth. 

fi the approximate time of birth is known, erect a 
chart for that time and from it cljoose the planet to work 
with which rules the house signifying the event or the 
person, as for instance: Rifler of the sevefith for the 
partner (marriage, business, or any other form of part- 
nership) ; ruler of fourth for father, ruler of tenth for 
mother, ruler of third for brother or sister, ruler of 
sixth for sickness and accidents of the querent, etc. 

Abbreviations: M. D., meridian distance; A. E., arc of 
event; R. A- M., right ascension of Midheaven; M. C., 
medium coeli or longitude of Midheaven; R. A., right 
ascension; S. T,, sidereal time. 

Use events whifh occurred before the age of thirty 
and always the first and most pronounced in its effect 
orf the native. When the event is death of a parent and 
Saturn is* found near the cusp of the tenth^or fourth, use 
it (Saturn) as significator of the event instead of the 
ruler of the parent's house, or if either Uranus, Saturn 
or Mars be in eighth house, us« it. When the event is 
a long /burner and change of residence and Uranus is 
found near cusp of* fourth Jhouse, t.*y it as the significator. 

1 Next find the A. E. as follows: 

• 

Ffom.the^year, month and date of the month in* which 
tlie event occurred subtract the date of birth, Convert 
the answer into degrees and minutes by allowing one 
degree for each yea r, five minutes for each month, and 
one. minute for each six days over a ‘’month.

Example 

Event 1895 March 13th or....l895— 3— 13 

Birth 1880 Jan. 1st or.. .1880—1 — 1 

15 Years, 2 mos. 12 days ..or..'. 15 — 2 — 12 
This answer converted would be 15* 12 # A. E. 

Next find'che R. A. (irrespective of the latitude) of the 
planet by which you wish to rectify. (Refer to Tabic of 
Right Ascensions on pages 518 to 521.) The R. A. of a 

S lanet in the 7th degree of the sign Leo is 129* 24'. The 
L A., or arc of a planet, is^ its distance from the 1st de- 
gree of Aries measured along the equatorial circle. 

If the planet in question be situated from the M. C. 
about an equal number of degrees to those constituting 
the arc of event then work it to a conjunction with the 
upper meridian. 

Conjunction With Upper Meridian 
Planet East 

If the planet is east of the upper' meridian, subtract 
the A. E. from thelR. A. of the planet, and the remainder 
will be the rectified R. A., or arc of Midheaven. Find this 
figure of R. A., or arc, in the Tables of Right Ascension 
and note what zodiacal sign and degree of longitude it 
represents \ then enter an appropriate Table of Houses, 
find that sign and degree on cusp of tenth house and fill 
in the other cusos of the chart from the tabte in the 
usual way. 

If the R. A. of the planet is too small to be subtracted' 
from, add 360 to it, then subtract the A. E. and use the 
remainder, which is the R. A. M. 

Conjunction With Upper Meridian 
Planet Weat 

If the planet is west of the upper meridian, add the 
A.i E. to the planet's right ascension, and the sum will 




PART IV, RECTIFICATION 511 

be the rectified R. A. M. Find this figure of R. A. in 
the -Tables of Right Ascensions »and note what zodiacal 
sign and degree of longitude it represents, then enter an 
appropriate Table *of Houses with that sign and degree 
on cusp of Tenth, House and fill in the other cusps of 
the chart from the tables in the usual manner. 

If after adding the A. E. to R. A. of the planet the 
sum comes to more than 360 (a complete ^circle), sub- 
tract 360 from it and use the remainder, which is the 
R. A. M. 

Conjunction With the liowcr Meridian 

If the planet in question is in the third, fourth or fifth 
house (providing it is nearer to the cusp of fifth than to 
cusp of sixth), it should be worked to a conjunction with 
the lower meridian. 

Planet East of Lower Meridian 

If the planet is east of the lower meridian, add the A. 
E. to the R. A. of the planet, then add 180 to. that and 

the sum will be the rectified R. A. M. 

• 

If after adding,* the sum exceeds#360, then subtract 
360 from it and use the remainder, which will be the R. 
A. M. 

Find this figure of R. A. in the Tables of Right Ascen- 
sions and note what zodiacal sign and degree it repre- 
sents; t^ien enter an appropriate Table of Houses with 
that sign and ^degree on cusp of tooth house and fill in 
the other cusps of the chVt from the table in the usual 
manner. 

Planet West of Lower Meridian 

If the planet is west of the lowe? meridian, subtract 
the A. E. from the R. A. of the planet, then add* 180 to 
that ana the sum will be the rectified R. A. M. 

• • 

It trie R. A. of the planet is too small tb be subtracted 
from, add 360 to it, "then subtract the A. E. To* the re- 
mainder add 180 and if the sijm exceeds 360'Subtract £60
